title: Add a Phone Number
lede: Get a phone number so your AI agents can make and take calls. You can buy a new number through iMash or import one you already own from another provider.

steps.title: Open Phone numbers
steps.content:
1. In the left menu, go to Call Center → Phone numbers.
  2. This page lists every number on your account with its label, capabilities, assigned agent, and status.

You have two ways to add one: Buy New Number (rented through iMash) or Import Number (a number you already own on your own carrier).

images.caption: Call Center → Phone numbers lists your numbers with Buy and Import buttons.
Call Center → Phone numbers lists your numbers with Buy and Import buttons.

steps.title: Buy a new number — search
steps.content:
1. Click Buy New Number.
  2. Choose a Country.
  3. Pick the capabilities you need — Voice is on by default (required for AI agent calls); add SMS, MMS, or Fax only if you need them (leaving them off gives the broadest results).
  4. In the search box, type an area code (e.g. 212), a city (e.g. London), or a prefix, then click Search.

images.caption: Search available numbers by country, capability, and area code or city.
Search available numbers by country, capability, and area code or city.

steps.title: Buy a new number — select and pay
steps.content:
1. Tick one or more numbers from the results (each row shows its monthly price).
  2. Choose a saved card to pay with. If you have no card yet, add one under Billing first.
  3. Some countries require identity documents — if a regulatory form appears, fill it in before buying.
  4. Click Buy Numbers. iMash purchases the number and sets up call routing automatically.

steps.title: Or import a number you own
steps.content:
1. Click Import Number.
  2. Select the Provider (your connected SIP carrier) — or click Add Provider to connect one first.
  3. Enter a Label and the phone number in E.164 form (for example +14155551234).
  4. Save. The number joins your list and routes through your provider's trunk.

steps.title: Point the number at an agent
steps.content: A new number won't answer calls until you assign an agent to it. Open the number (click it in the list) or select it and use the bulk action to set an Inbound Agent — see the Call Routing guide next.

nextSteps.title: What's Next?
nextSteps.description: Now decide what happens when someone calls the number.

cards.title: Route Calls to an Agent
cards.description: Assign an inbound agent so the number answers calls.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaTitle: Add a Phone Number - Buy or Import a Number | iMash Docs
metaDescription: Buy a new local, toll-free, or international number through iMash, or import a number you already own. Then assign it to an AI agent.
